name: Mct's Conch Chowder
recipeIngredient: 6 oz Bacon, cut into 1/4 inch dice
1 8 oz. can Diced tomatoes in juice
2 lb Conch or clams, diced (see Note)
1 T Thyme, use fresh if available
2 oz Hot sauce or salsa (see Note)
recipeInstructions:
1. Saute bacon until soft-cooked.
2. Add green peppers and onion to the skillet and saute to al dente stage.
3. Place in a large pot and add tomato puree, diced tomatoes, clam juice, salt, pepper, bay leaf, thyme, potatoes, jalapeno, hot sauce, and conch.
4. Bring to a low boil for 15 minutes.
5. Simmer for 1 hour.
6. To hold until serving time, cool to room temperature and refrigerate.
Note: At McT's, the diced conch meat is marinated overnight in papaya juice to tenderize it. The papaya juice can be rinsed off with a little left in to give a slight sweet flavor to the chowder. In the restaurant, they use Pico Pica hot sauce, usually only available to the trade, but La Victoria hot salsa, which is more available, may be substituted.
